Analyst+Diplomat.speaker: A
lines: {A} is already reciting three statistics nobody asked for. {B} listens patiently.
reply: What you're saying is correct, {A}, but maybe we don't need the numbers to feel it.

Analyst+Sentinel.speaker: A
lines: {A} corrects a data point in {B}'s report for the second time.
reply: The protocol doesn't need to be more precise, {A}, just respected.

Explorer+Analyst.speaker: A
lines: {A} already jumps to the next conclusion. {B} hasn't finished the calculation.
reply: Let me finish, {A}, I was almost there.

Diplomat+Sentinel.speaker: A
lines: {A} suggests hearing the other side out. {B} sees no tactical point in it.

Explorer+Diplomat.speaker: A
lines: {A} wants to charge ahead without discussing it. {B} would rather talk it through first.
reply: Can we at least talk about it first, {A}?

Explorer+Sentinel.speaker: A
lines: {A} improvises, again. {B} mentally notes the protocol deviation.

Analyst+Analyst.speaker: A
lines: {A} disputes {B}'s interpretation of the numbers, politely but firmly.
reply: Your data is good, {A}, but your reading is incomplete.

Diplomat+Diplomat.speaker: A
lines: {A} lets {B} speak first. {B} insists it should be the other way around.
reply: No, no, you go ahead, {A}, please.

Sentinel+Sentinel.speaker: A
lines: {A} and {B} disagree on which of them is actually running the operation.
reply: I'm the one with the current protocol, {A}, not you.

Explorer+Explorer.speaker: A
lines: {A} proposes an increasingly questionable plan. {B} thinks it's brilliant.
